frameworks.name: Decoded Neurofeedback (DecNef)
frameworks.one_line: implicit regulation of multivariate fMRI voxel patterns tied to feared stimuli

frameworks.name: EEG/fMRI conventional neurofeedback
frameworks.one_line: feedback based on signals averaged within specific brain areas

frameworks.name: Exposure-based therapy
frameworks.one_line: current first-line PTSD treatment with high drop-out due to aversiveness

frameworks.name: Counter conditioning
frameworks.one_line: competing-association account contrasted with exposure in a mathematical model

core_question: Can decoded neurofeedback ameliorate PTSD where conventional neurofeedback and aversive exposure fall short?
key_assumptions: multivariate voxel patterns can index trauma-specific neural representations
implicit pattern regulation can substitute for explicit exposure
exposure and counter conditioning produce dissociable effects in models
manipulations: type of neurofeedback (DecNef vs conventional EEG/fMRI)
application of DecNef to PTSD patients
key_findings: systematic review compared DecNef against conventional EEG/fMRI neurofeedback for PTSD
mathematical modeling indicated exposure is the key component of DecNef effects
DecNef in four PTSD patients produced significant reduction in PTSD severity
interpretations: DecNef can bypass conscious aversive exposure while still engaging its therapeutic mechanism
implicit multivariate pattern regulation is a plausible novel PTSD intervention
limitations_noted: very small clinical pilot sample (four patients)
